answer:
Honestly, one of my biggest goals for the next year is to get accepted into a top university in the United States. I think it's something I've been working toward for a while, and, you know, it feels really important to me. To achieve this, I've been putting a lot of effort into my academics—like, I spend extra time studying for my classes and I even joined a couple of advanced courses last semester to sort of challenge myself. Also, I've tried to be active outside of the classroom, so I participate in community service and I'm part of a debate club, which, I mean, really helps sharpen my communication skills.

Applying to universities in the U.S. can be pretty demanding, so I've been preparing for standardized tests like the SAT and TOEFL. For example, I practice writing essays and speaking English every day, and sometimes I'll watch videos or listen to podcasts to improve my fluency. My teachers have been super supportive, giving me feedback and tips, which motivates me even more.

I think that if I manage to achieve this goal, it’ll make me a much better person. Not only will it open doors academically, but it'll also help me grow as an individual—living in a different country means I'll learn to be independent and adaptable. Plus, interacting with people from diverse backgrounds will, well, broaden my perspective and teach me to be more empathetic. So, accomplishing this isn’t just about academics; it’s really about becoming someone who’s resilient, open-minded, and ready to take on challenges, both in college and beyond.
title: Name a goal that you want to achieve in the next year. What are you doing to achieve this goal? How will accomplishing this goal make you a better person?
